title: Deep Learning for accelerated debugging in Design Verification and Post Silicon Validation of Scandump design
authors: Shreya Jayateerth Joshi, Poonam M Shettar, Sanjoy Saha, Shrinidhi S. Rao, Alok Kumar, Garima Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: As modern System-on-chip (SoC) designs grow in complexity, traditional debug mechanisms involving manual waveform and log analysis struggle to keep up with the volume and intricacies of failures. Complex SoCs integrate multiple application-specific processors posing a challenge in the debugging phase of Design Verification (DV) and Post-Silicon Validation (PSV). We propose Artificial Intelligence (AI) augmented verification strategies to enhance debugging and improve verification efficiency. The proposed work uses Deep learning models alongside SystemVerilog (SV) based assertions in test bench (TB) and Python routines. By leveraging the capability of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to remember sequential data, we attempt to simplify the bus protocol error debug in DV. We observe that the Long short-term memory (LSTM) algorithm consistently outperforms the RNN algorithm in terms of accuracy in bus error detection and classification. LSTM Autoencoder is used for anomaly detection in waveform mining, which is then used to identify required TB or design updates through Python routine. An integrated dashboard improves the efficacy of using this AI-Machine Learning (ML) based solution for debugging. Overall, with this approach, we observed that verification effort became 0.54x.
title_zh: 基于深度学习的扫描设计在设计验证与硅后验证中的加速调试方法
abstract_zh: 随着现代片上系统（SoC）设计复杂度的不断提升，传统的调试机制——依赖人工波形和日志分析——已难以应对故障数量庞大且结构复杂的挑战。复杂的SoC集成了多个专用应用处理器，这给设计验证（DV）和硅后验证（PSV）阶段的调试带来了巨大困难。为此，我们提出了一种基于人工智能（AI）增强的验证策略，以提升调试效率并优化验证流程。该方法结合深度学习模型、基于SystemVerilog（SV）的断言以及Python脚本，在测试平台（TB）中实现智能分析。通过利用循环神经网络（RNN）对序列数据的记忆能力，我们尝试简化DV阶段中的总线协议错误调试。实验结果表明，长短期记忆网络（LSTM）在总线错误检测与分类的准确性方面始终优于传统RNN。此外，采用LSTM自编码器进行波形挖掘中的异常检测，并通过Python脚本进一步识别出需要更新的测试平台或设计部分。一个集成的可视化仪表盘显著提升了该AI-机器学习（ML）解决方案在实际调试中的使用效率。总体而言，采用该方法后，验证工作量降低了54%（即验证效率提升至原来的0.54倍）。

title: Decoding Cyber Threats: Advancement in AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection
authors: Shahriar Arefin Zummon, Fahim Ahmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Web application vulnerabilities are omnipresent and a source of critical threat in our digital data driven world, demanding more advanced defense mechanisms. This paper presents a groundbreaking comparative study that puts the power of advanced transformer-based deep learning to the test against six major vulnerability types: SQL Injection, XSS, CSRF, SSRF, Clickjacking, and Broken Authentication. Through rigorous evaluation of six state-of-the-art models from general-purpose giants such as BERT to specialized, code-aware architectures-we demonstrate that GraphCodeBERT emerges as the best option, achieving a near-perfect 99.57% accuracy by leveraging graph-based representations to understand the code structure; all the while significantly outperforming other models including its cousin CodeBERT (95.21%) and further proving that how a model "sees" the code is of paramount significance. Our analysis reveals that while certain threats like CSRF and Clickjacking are detected with 100% precision across the board, we also identify DistilBERT as offering an impressive 98.06% accuracy with remarkable efficiency for resource-conscious environments. By integrating CVSS severity scores and LIME explain ability, we provided not just detection but interpretable and actionable insights, achieving 100% recall on critical vulnerabilities. This work positively shows that code specific, structure aware Al is the future of automated security, providing a blueprint for selecting optimal models that balance cutting-edge accuracy with practical efficiency to strengthen the next generation of production security systems.
title_zh: 解码网络威胁：人工智能驱动漏洞检测的进展
abstract_zh: Web应用漏洞在我们这个以数据驱动的数字世界中无处不在，构成了严重的安全威胁，亟需更先进的防御机制。本文开展了一项开创性的对比研究，将基于先进Transformer架构的深度学习模型应用于六类主要漏洞的检测：SQL注入、跨站脚本（XSS）、跨站请求伪造（CSRF）、服务器端请求伪造（SSRF）、点击劫持（Clickjacking）以及身份认证缺陷。通过对来自通用大型模型（如BERT）到专为代码设计的感知架构等六种前沿模型进行严格评估，我们发现GraphCodeBERT表现最佳，通过利用基于图的代码结构表示，实现了接近完美的99.57%准确率；同时显著优于其他模型，包括其同源模型CodeBERT（准确率为95.21%），进一步证明了模型“理解”代码的方式具有决定性意义。我们的分析显示，尽管某些威胁如CSRF和点击劫持在所有模型中均达到100%的检测精度，但我们也发现DistilBERT在资源受限环境中展现出惊人的效率与性能，准确率达到98.06%。通过结合CVSS严重性评分与LIME可解释性分析，我们不仅实现了漏洞检测，还提供了可解释且可操作的安全洞察，在关键漏洞上实现了100%的召回率。本研究有力表明，面向代码、具备结构感知能力的AI是自动化安全的未来方向，为选择在尖端准确率与实际效率之间取得平衡的最优模型提供了蓝图，有助于构建下一代生产环境中的安全防护体系。

title: Using Artificial Intelligence as an Assistant in Information Systems Security Testing
authors: Vasili Vaskevich, Maksim Vinokurov, Tatsiana Melnikova, Daniil Shumilo
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper provides an overview of the main functions of popular generative artificial intelligence chatbots: ChatGPT, Grok, DeepSeek, Gemini, and Copilot. Examples of the use of artificial intelligence in modern cybersecurity software solutions are considered. The results of using artificial intelligence as an assistant in vulnerability testing and analysis of information security logs are presented. The paper determines how these chatbots can assist novice information security specialists in identifying necessary actions for exploiting vulnerabilities and searching for suspicious entries in the saved logs of attacked servers. The main advantages and disadvantages of using AI-powered chatbots in the cybersecurity field are described.
title_zh: 使用人工智能作为信息系统的安全测试助手
abstract_zh: 本文概述了主流生成式人工智能聊天机器人——ChatGPT、Grok、DeepSeek、Gemini和Copilot的主要功能。文章还探讨了人工智能在现代网络安全软件解决方案中的应用实例。研究展示了将人工智能作为助手用于漏洞测试及信息安全日志分析的成果。论文进一步阐明了这些聊天机器人如何帮助初学者信息安全专业人员识别利用漏洞所需的操作，并查找被攻击服务器日志中可疑的条目。最后，文章总结了在网络安全领域使用AI驱动聊天机器人的主要优势与局限性。

title: Enabling Multi-fabric Net Tracing: A Step Towards Holistic EDA System Integration
authors: Shweta Kumari, Rohit Kumar, Vidhi Bansal, Tapan Kumar Singh, Amit Kumar, Rudy Couget
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: As modern electronic systems grow in complexity, driven by advancements in heterogeneous integration, they present significant challenges in ensuring seamless connectivity across multiple design fabrics, encompassing silicon IC, package substrates, printed circuit boards (PCBs), and multiple inter-connected ICs or sub-fabrics. The Electronic Design Automation (EDA) industry is driven to develop unified, system-aware tools. This paper discusses the necessity of multi-fabric net tracing (tracing a single net across multiple IC sub-fabrics, package, and board) and how such capabilities address critical system-level design bottlenecks. We highlight current EDA limitations, propose a solution for integrated tracing support, and argue its significance for faster verification, better co-design, and improved manufacturability.
title_zh: 启用多 Fabric 网络追踪：迈向全面的 EDA 系统集成
abstract_zh: 随着现代电子系统复杂性的不断提升，得益于异构集成技术的进步，其在确保跨多种设计结构（包括硅基集成电路、封装基板、印刷电路板（PCB）以及多个互连的集成电路或子结构）之间的无缝连接方面面临巨大挑战。电子设计自动化（EDA）行业亟需开发统一且具备系统感知能力的工具。本文探讨了多结构网络追踪（即跨多个集成电路子结构、封装和印制板追踪单个网络）的必要性，并阐述此类功能如何解决关键的系统级设计瓶颈。文章指出了当前EDA工具的局限性，提出了一种集成化追踪支持的解决方案，并论证了该技术对于加快验证速度、提升协同设计水平以及改善可制造性的重大意义。

title: MIRAI: Multimodal Intelligence via Reflective agentic AI for enterprise knowledge assistants
authors: Priyadarshini Panemangalore Pai, Subrata Sarkar, Vemuri Krishna Sumanth, Vijaya Kirti Gupta, Garima Srivastava, Venkata Subba Rao Manne
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Secure and efficient knowledge management based on enterprise chip design and verification documents or bug trackers, pose unique challenges, due to their complex, sensitive, heterogeneous, and multimodal nature. Rapid miniaturization of nodes, sophistication of Intellectual Property (IP)s, and evolution of system-on-chip (SoC) architectures have further added to the demanding scenario, where, within the available enterprise infrastructure, effective solutions have to be developed based on context and requirements of various operational settings. Available state-of-the-art solutions have limited scope of sensitivity to organizational dynamics and smart computational resource planning. In this paper, we present MIRAI- a Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrated Artificial Intelligence (AI) framework that combines language, vision, and structured data understanding through a reflective agentic architecture to provide real-time, contextual and explainable support in Design Verification and Verification (DV&V) tasks. The proposed system has been evaluated across use cases, demonstrating significant gains, i.e., up to 90-95% accuracy in information retrieval, in secure policy information access, AI-based query answering, anomaly detection, debugging, and knowledge mining and functional testing with improved coverage. We believe MIRAI offers a transformative approach to managing and leveraging design data, engineering workflows, and cross-functional team intelligence.
title_zh: MIRAI：通过反思性代理人工智能实现多模态智能的企业知识助手
abstract_zh: 基于企业芯片设计与验证文档或缺陷追踪系统的安全高效知识管理，因其复杂性、敏感性、异构性和多模态特性而面临独特挑战。随着制程节点的快速微缩、知识产权（IP）的日益复杂化以及系统级芯片（SoC）架构的持续演进，这一挑战进一步加剧。在现有企业基础设施条件下，必须根据各类运营场景的具体上下文与需求，开发出有效的解决方案。当前最先进的技术方案在应对组织动态变化方面敏感度有限，且在智能计算资源规划方面能力不足。本文提出MIRAI——一种集成模型上下文协议（MCP）的人工智能（AI）框架，通过反射式代理架构融合语言、视觉与结构化数据理解能力，为设计验证（DV&V）任务提供实时、上下文感知且可解释的支持。该系统已在多个应用场景中进行评估，表现出显著成效：信息检索准确率高达90%-95%，在安全策略信息访问、基于AI的查询应答、异常检测、调试、知识挖掘及功能测试等方面均实现覆盖率提升。我们相信，MIRAI为管理与利用设计数据、工程工作流程以及跨职能团队智慧提供了一种变革性的方法。

title: RAG-Enhanced Multi-Model Ensemble for Automated Vulnerability Detection Using SLMs
authors: Fateha Jannat Ayrin, Mizanur Rahman, Sujan Kumar Bhowmik, Syeda Sadia Alam, Vishwanath Akuthota, Masud Mohiuddin, Md Maruf Hasan Khondaker, Muntasim Ul Haque, Montaser Abdul Quader
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Automated vulnerability detection in source code is essential for modern software security, yet existing approaches suffer from high false positive rates, weak contextual understanding, and limited recall. This work presents the most comprehensive empirical evaluation to date of Small Language Model (SLM) based vulnerability detection, spanning more than twenty system configurations ranging from simple prompting baselines to advanced retrieval-augmented ensembles. Our methodology systematically examines how small LLMs (1–3B parameters) behave under different prompting strategies, static analysis integrations, ensembling techniques, and retrieval mechanisms. Through extensive ablation studies on the DiverseVul dataset, we find that many commonly assumed improvements such as complex Chain-of-Thought prompting, multi-model aggregation, or post-processing verification frequently degrade performance, largely due to overfitting and the inherent reasoning limitations of small models. In contrast, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) consistently enhances both recall and stability, providing an average F1 gain of 4–5% by grounding model predictions in relevant vulnerability examples. Our best-performing system, a RAG-enhanced stacking ensemble with structured reasoning, achieves a 71.2% F1-score, surpassing the state-of-the-art LLaMA 3.2 model by 5.2 percentage points. our findings demonstrate that effective vulnerability detection does not arise from increased architectural complexity but from combining lightweight models with robust retrieval and context-aware reasoning. This study establishes RAG-driven small LLMs as a practical, cost-efficient, and empirically validated direction for secure software analysis. our source code at https://github.com/rafi79/RAG-Enhanced-Multi-Model-Ensemble-for-Automated-Vulnerability-Detection-Using-SLMs.
title_zh: 基于小语言模型的RAG增强型多模型集成自动化漏洞检测
abstract_zh: 源代码中的自动化漏洞检测对于现代软件安全至关重要，然而现有方法普遍存在误报率高、上下文理解能力弱以及召回率有限等问题。本文首次对基于小型语言模型（SLM）的漏洞检测进行了迄今为止最全面的实证评估，涵盖了超过二十种系统配置，从简单的提示基线到先进的检索增强集成方法。我们的研究方法系统地考察了小型语言模型（1–3B参数量）在不同提示策略、静态分析集成、集成技术以及检索机制下的表现。通过对DiverseVul数据集进行广泛的消融实验，我们发现许多普遍认为能提升性能的方法，如复杂的思维链（Chain-of-Thought）提示、多模型聚合或后处理验证，实际上常常导致性能下降，主要原因在于过拟合以及小型模型固有的推理局限性。相比之下，检索增强生成（RAG）方法始终能够显著提升召回率与稳定性，通过将模型预测锚定在相关的漏洞示例上，平均带来4–5%的F1分数提升。我们表现最佳的系统——一种结合结构化推理的RAG增强堆叠集成模型——取得了71.2%的F1分数，比当前最先进的LLaMA 3.2模型高出5.2个百分点。我们的研究结果表明，有效的漏洞检测并非源于架构复杂度的增加，而是通过将轻量级模型与强大的检索机制及上下文感知推理相结合实现的。本研究确立了以RAG驱动的小型语言模型作为安全软件分析的一种实用、低成本且经实证验证的新方向。相关源代码可访问：https://github.com/rafi79/RAG-Enhanced-Multi-Model-Ensemble-for-Automated-Vulnerability-Detection-Using-SLMs。

title: Development of a Simplified Multi-Agent System for Fault Diagnosis
authors: Chunzhi Wu, Hua Zhong, Zhixin Jiao, Gaihong Yuan, Shuqi Hu, Jianyi Zhang, Rujun Sun, Chunyan Peng, Yougao Xiao
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: We present a lightweight, locally deployable agentic framework for fault diagnosis that treats large language models (LLMs) as controllers to orchestrate domain tools (feature extraction, visualization, and classifiers) under a unified workflow. Our goal is architectural and operational: to demonstrate how an LLM-driven pipeline enables traceable reasoning, plug-and-play tools, and maintainable workflows for PHM. We do not pursue head-to-head model comparisons. Instead, the framework is model-agnostic and uses best-of-breed, off-the-shelf models for each subtask, focusing on (i) natural-language tasking by engineers, (ii) on-demand tool orchestration, (iii) auditable, stepwise decision traces, and (iv) low-friction updates via prompt/tool changes rather than retraining. A case study illustrates end-to-end outputs that tie indicators, figures, and predictions to actionable maintenance suggestions, showing the framework’s value in real operations where data, tooling, and requirements evolve. The corresponding source code is publicly available on GitHub: https://github.com/chuznhiwu/dynamic_langgraph.
title_zh: 一种简化多智能体系统在故障诊断中的开发
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种轻量级、可本地部署的智能体框架，用于故障诊断，将大型语言模型（LLMs）作为控制器，统一调度领域工具（特征提取、可视化和分类器）在标准化工作流中协同运行。我们的目标在于架构与操作层面：展示基于LLM的流水线如何实现可追溯的推理过程、即插即用的工具集成，以及可维护的工作流程，以支持预测与健康管理（PHM）。我们不进行模型间的直接性能对比。相反，该框架具备模型无关性，针对每个子任务采用业界最佳的现成模型，重点聚焦于：(i) 工程师通过自然语言下达任务指令；(ii) 按需动态编排工具；(iii) 可审计的、分步决策的追踪记录；(iv) 通过修改提示词或工具即可实现低门槛更新，无需重新训练。一个案例研究展示了从指标、图表到预测结果的端到端输出，并将其与可操作的维护建议相联系，充分体现了该框架在实际运维中的价值——尤其在数据、工具和需求持续演进的场景下。相关源代码已公开发布于GitHub：https://github.com/chuznhiwu/dynamic_langgraph。

title: Enhancing debugging efficiency in bug resolution -The role of AI based Triage Accelerator
authors: G Rashmi, Ritu Garg, Aniket Kashyap, H K Meghana, Nishant Kumar, Shivam
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Efficient defect triage is essential for maintaining product quality and accelerating time-to-market in complex hardware, software, and firmware development environments. Traditional triage processes are manual, time-consuming, and heavily reliant on expert knowledge, often leading to delays and inconsistencies in defect resolution. This paper presents the Triage Accelerator (TA) Tool, an AI-driven framework that automates and streamlines the defect management lifecycle. TA leverages historical defect data, advanced machine learning models, and generative AI to provide real-time recommendations for defect signature extraction, exposure assessment, component identification, and triage summary generation. TA tool demonstrates high accuracy (exceeding $80 \%$) in both experimental and live validation scenarios, significantly reducing triage latency from several days to a few hours.
title_zh: 提升缺陷修复中调试效率——基于人工智能的缺陷分类加速器的作用
abstract_zh: 在复杂的硬件、软件和固件开发环境中，高效的缺陷分类对于保障产品质量以及加速产品上市至关重要。传统的缺陷分类流程依赖人工操作，耗时且高度依赖专家经验，常常导致缺陷处理延迟和不一致。本文提出了一种名为“Triage Accelerator（TA）”的工具，这是一个由人工智能驱动的框架，能够自动化并优化缺陷管理的全生命周期。TA工具利用历史缺陷数据、先进的机器学习模型以及生成式AI，实时提供缺陷特征提取、影响范围评估、组件识别及分类摘要生成等方面的建议。在实验和实际生产环境中的验证均表明，TA工具在缺陷分类任务中表现出超过80%的高准确率，将缺陷分类的延迟从数天显著缩短至数小时。

title: Open-Source Emulation-based AI Framework for Performance Verification in RISC-V SoCs
authors: Gorle Pooja, Gummadi Kumar Raju, Kandyana Dharani, M Chinnadurai
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: The increasing complexity of RISC-V System-on-Chips (SoCs) necessitates scalable and efficient performance verification methodologies. A verification-intensive approach like this demands more than what traditional simulation-based techniques offer and calls for a unified, open-source, emulation-based Al-integrated verification framework. This is aimed at early detection and analysis of performance bottlenecks such as as bandwidth congestion. The current work proposes an analogous infrastructure centered around a modular RISC-V processor that can be simply implemented on Xilinx Vivado, consisting of instruction fetch, decode, ALU, and register file units. A robust addition to this strategy are precisely positioned performance monitors (PMs) to capture essential metrics like throughput, latency, and outstanding buffer depth in real time, which are modeled to guide test refinement and facilitate stream-lined debugging. This structure is augmented with machine learning (ML) models, namely XGBoost, which are trained on the captured metric data to facilitate prediction of input test combinations yielding worst-case scenarios to improve corner-case coverage. This methodology can be validated on open-source RISC-V cores like CVA-6, Rocket Chip, etc., using tools such as Verilator, Renode and FireSim. The efficacy of the discussed structure lies in its high observability, faster turnaround time, and accuracy, delivering practicality in terms of academic research and industrial design to the work under discussion.
title_zh: 基于开源模拟的AI框架，用于RISC-V SoC的性能验证
abstract_zh: 随着RISC-V片上系统（SoC）复杂度的不断提升，亟需可扩展且高效的性能验证方法。此类验证强度较高的方法已超越传统基于仿真的技术所能提供的能力，迫切需要一个统一、开源、基于仿真加速的AI集成验证框架，以实现对性能瓶颈（如带宽拥塞）的早期检测与分析。本文提出了一种类似的基础设施，其核心是一个模块化的RISC-V处理器，该处理器可简单地在Xilinx Vivado平台上实现，包含指令取指、解码、算术逻辑单元（ALU）和寄存器文件等基本单元。该方案的一大亮点是精确部署的性能监控单元（PMs），能够实时捕获吞吐量、延迟以及待处理缓冲区深度等关键指标，这些数据被建模用于指导测试用例的优化，并支持高效、简化的调试流程。此外，该架构还集成了机器学习（ML）模型，特别是XGBoost模型，利用采集到的性能数据进行训练，从而预测可能导致最坏情况的输入测试组合，有效提升边缘场景的覆盖率。该方法可在开源RISC-V核（如CVA-6、Rocket Chip等）上通过Verilator、Renode或FireSim等工具进行验证。所提出的结构具有高可观测性、快速的反馈周期和高精度，兼具学术研究价值与工业设计实用性，为相关工作提供了切实可行的解决方案。

title: Enhancing Autonomous Vehicle Test Scenario Reasoning in Language Models
authors: Denise Ataei, Ishaan Paranjape, Jim Whitehead
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Scenario-based testing is a promising approach to evaluate autonomous vehicles for safety due to its ability to evaluate several components at once. Automated generation of these scenarios in simulation is needed to address the scale and diversity requirements in scenarios. Large Language Models (LLMs) can address this need due to their ability for world modeling. However, these models are ineffective in their reasoning thereby limiting their abilities in generating complex, dynamic vehicle interaction scenarios. In this paper, we present Cruzway Scenario Reasoner, an LLM based system that enhances reasoning capabilities of language models for complex vehicle interaction questions from the Waymo Open Motion Dataset Reasoning dataset. This system consists of a suite of prompting approaches which include both Chain of Thought prompting as well as prompting based on model-based task planning in the Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL). In addition, this system contains LLM as a judge modules for the effective evaluation of generated responses and PDDL models. With this system, we are able to elevate the reasoning capabilities of the OpenAI GPT 4o-mini model. We also provide detailed qualitative analysis of the language model responses to 15 scenarios categorized by complexity in the information provided.
title_zh: 增强语言模型在自动驾驶车辆测试场景推理方面的能力
abstract_zh: 情境化测试是一种极具前景的自动驾驶车辆安全评估方法，因其能够同时评估多个系统组件而备受关注。为满足测试场景在规模和多样性方面的需求，亟需在仿真环境中实现这些场景的自动化生成。大型语言模型（LLMs）由于具备世界建模能力，有望解决这一需求。然而，这些模型在推理能力方面表现不足，限制了其生成复杂、动态车辆交互场景的能力。本文提出了一种基于大语言模型的系统——Cruzway Scenario Reasoner，旨在增强语言模型在处理来自Waymo Open Motion Dataset Reasoning数据集中的复杂车辆交互问题时的推理能力。该系统包含一系列提示策略，包括链式思维（Chain of Thought）提示以及基于规划领域定义语言（PDDL）的模型化任务规划提示。此外，系统还集成了一个以大语言模型作为评判模块的机制，用于对生成的回答及PDDL模型进行有效评估。通过该系统，我们显著提升了OpenAI GPT-4o-mini模型的推理能力。同时，本文还针对15个按信息复杂度分类的典型场景，提供了详细的定性分析，深入探讨了语言模型在不同复杂程度情境下的响应表现。

title: EAGLE-X: Cross-Language Code Clone Detection via Enhanced Graph Learning and Dynamic Feature Fusion
authors: Boshu Wang, Chengwan He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Cross-language code clone detection (CLCD) is a critical task for ensuring the quality and consistency of multi-lingual software systems, yet state-of-the-art methods still face significant challenges. Sequence-based approaches often fail to capture explicit code structures, while graph-based methods are hampered by the structural heterogeneity and noise introduced by linguistic differences, and often lack effective mechanisms for multi-modal feature fusion.To address these limitations, we propose EAGLE-X, a novel multi-modal deep learning model. EAGLE-X employs a dual-path architecture to extract sequence and structure features in parallel. The sequence path utilizes the pre-trained model CodeBERT. The structure path leverages tree-sitter to generate a Concrete Syntax Tree (CST), which is then converted into a Semantic-Enhanced Graph via our designed normalization and enhancement strategies and encoded by a Graph Attention Network (GAT). We further introduce a dynamic feature fusion module that adaptively learns to combine multiple fusion strategies to optimally integrate the two modalities. The entire model is optimized end-to-end with a contrastive learning objective to learn a representation space that is robust to linguistic differences.Experiments on a public benchmark dataset indicate that EAGLE-X achieves competitive performance compared to existing state-of-the-art baselines. Furthermore, comprehensive ablation studies confirm that both our proposed semantic-enhanced graph and the dynamic fusion module make effective contributions to the model’s final performance.
title_zh: EAGLE-X：通过增强图学习与动态特征融合实现跨语言代码克隆检测
abstract_zh: 跨语言代码克隆检测（CLCD）是保障多语言软件系统质量与一致性的一项关键任务，然而当前最先进的方法仍面临诸多挑战。基于序列的方法往往难以捕捉代码的显式结构，而基于图的方法则受限于语言差异带来的结构异质性和噪声，且通常缺乏有效的多模态特征融合机制。为解决上述局限性，我们提出了一种新型的多模态深度学习模型——EAGLE-X。EAGLE-X采用双路径架构，可并行提取序列特征与结构特征：序列路径利用预训练模型CodeBERT；结构路径则通过Tree-sitter生成具体语法树（CST），再经由我们设计的归一化与增强策略转换为语义增强图，并由图注意力网络（GAT）进行编码。此外，我们引入了一个动态特征融合模块，能够自适应地学习多种融合策略，以最优方式整合两种模态的信息。整个模型通过对比学习目标进行端到端优化，从而学习到对语言差异具有鲁棒性的表示空间。在公开基准数据集上的实验表明，EAGLE-X在性能上优于现有主流基线方法。此外，全面的消融实验证实，我们提出的语义增强图以及动态融合模块均对模型最终性能作出了显著贡献。

title: A Technical Overview of Continuous and Discrete Diffusion-based Language Models
authors: Haiyang Zhao, Jian Huang, Shangsong Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Diffusion-based language models (DLMs) have recently emerged as a non-autoregressive paradigm that departs from the strictly sequential generation of traditional language models. By iteratively refining noisy text representations in parallel, DLMs achieve faster inference and improved global coherence. The adaptation of this framework to discrete language data has resulted in two primary technical branches: Continuous Diffusion Models, which operate in a continuous embedding space, and Discrete Diffusion Models, which define the process directly on the token vocabulary. This survey provides a focused technical review of the core implementation aspects that underpin these two paradigms. We systematically deconstruct and compare foundational topics, including the methods for handling discrete variables ; the formulation of diffusion steps ; the design of noise schedules ; the evolution of objective functions for stable training ; and various sampling techniques for generation. By presenting a detailed analysis of these components, this work serves as a technical guide for researchers and practitioners, clarifying the principles and trade-offs essential for advancing this promising field.
title_zh: 基于连续与离散扩散的语言模型技术综述
abstract_zh: 基于扩散的语言模型（DLMs）最近作为一种非自回归范式崭露头角，其不再遵循传统语言模型严格的序列生成方式。通过并行地迭代优化带有噪声的文本表示，DLMs 实现了更快的推理速度和更优的全局连贯性。该框架在离散语言数据上的适配催生了两大核心技术分支：连续扩散模型（Continuous Diffusion Models），在连续嵌入空间中运行；以及离散扩散模型（Discrete Diffusion Models），直接在词元词汇空间中定义扩散过程。本文对支撑这两种范式的底层实现技术进行了聚焦性的综述。我们系统地剖析并比较了基础性议题，包括离散变量的处理方法、扩散步骤的建模方式、噪声调度的设计策略、用于稳定训练的目标函数演化，以及各类生成采样技术。通过对这些核心组件的深入分析，本文为研究人员与实践者提供了一份技术指南，阐明了推动这一前景广阔的领域所必需的核心原理与权衡取舍。

title: AI Agent Debug: Transforming Silicon Hardware debug with Machine Learning and GenAI
authors: Trupti Joshi, Nakul Choudhari, Ashwani Kumar, Vishal B Bhogade
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Managing hardware and software failures in large-scale systems is a critical challenge that demands efficient and accurate automated solutions. This paper presents a comprehensive automated failure management workflow leveraging Generative AI (GenAI) and cloud-based clustering models. The proposed system integrates data extraction, clustering, similarity analysis, and failure resolution into a unified framework using advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques. The workflow comprises two main components: (1) comprehensive failure database creation via APIs and clustering models, and (2) daily failure analysis generating actionable insights and automated recommendations. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in processing efficiency, handling 800+ records daily with 92% precision and 88% recall, showcasing GenAI’s transformative potential in intelligent error analysis systems for modern computing infrastructure.
title_zh: AI代理调试：利用机器学习与生成式AI革新硅硬件调试
abstract_zh: 在大规模系统中管理硬件和软件故障是一项关键挑战，需要高效且准确的自动化解决方案。本文提出了一种全面的自动化故障管理流程，该流程结合生成式人工智能（GenAI）与基于云的聚类模型。所提出的系统利用先进的自然语言处理（NLP）技术，将数据提取、聚类分析、相似性检测与故障解决整合为一个统一框架。该工作流包含两个主要组成部分：（1）通过API接口和聚类模型构建全面的故障数据库；（2）每日故障分析，生成可操作的洞察并提供自动化建议。实验结果表明，该系统在处理效率方面取得显著提升，每日可处理800余条记录，达到92%的精确率和88%的召回率，充分展示了生成式人工智能在现代计算基础设施智能错误分析系统中的变革性潜力。
